title: EN-language recommendations return JA summary_table field labels
description:
When generate-recommendation is called with language='en', the AI correctly emits final_recommendation in English, but summary_table[].field labels come back in Japanese (e.g., '価格', '通勤時間', '間取り', 'カフェの近さ' instead of 'Price', 'Commute', 'Layout', 'Cafés nearby').

Confirmed via local edge function smoke test on 2026-05-27:
- POST http://127.0.0.1:54321/functions/v1/generate-recommendation with language='en'
- Gemini 2.0 Flash-Lite returns 9 summary_table rows
- final_recommendation: English ('I recommend Property A in Nakameguro...')
- summary_table[].field: Japanese ('価格', '通勤時間', '間取り', 'カフェの近さ', 'ジムへのアクセス', '犬の散歩', '夜の静けさ', '日当たり', 'コインランドリー')

Pre-existing behavior (not introduced by bead 2l8). Likely cause: the EN prompt in supabase/functions/generate-recommendation/index.ts shows English example labels in the JSON sample but the badge rule ('1-3 Japanese characters, examples: 安, 広, 近') and example values pull the AI toward emitting everything in Japanese.

Impact: EN-locale users see a mixed-language report — English narrative + Japanese table labels. Looks unfinished.

Likely fix: tighten the EN prompt so badges and field labels are explicitly required to be in English for language='en', or split badge guidance per-locale. Test by re-running the EN smoke payload and asserting all summary_table[].field values match the English example set.

Local edge runtime had flaky DNS on a second EN attempt — environmental, not code.

title: Deferred polish for nae SSR comparison route (cleanup)
description:
Non-blocking quality cleanups surfaced during the nae (rv5-b) review loop and intentionally deferred so they wouldn't gate the P0 SSR merge. All scoped to the files nae touched (src/pages/ComparisonDetail.tsx, src/root.tsx, src/lib/site.ts). Do AFTER nae (PR #9) is merged.

ITEMS:
1. ComparisonDetail loader — collapse the three scattered inline casts ((row as { view_count?: number }).view_count, image_extraction_status, save_count) into a single narrowing at the top of the mapping block.
2. SITE_TITLE/SITE_DESC dedup — root.tsx meta() declares SITE_TITLE locally; ComparisonDetail's meta() 404 fallback hard-codes the same literal 'AiSumai (愛住) - Compare Homes in Japan with AI & Experts'. Lift to a shared const (e.g. src/lib/site.ts) and import in both to remove drift risk.
3. Recommendation newest-pick — loader does [...recommendations].sort(...)[0]; replace with a linear reduce max-pick (avoids the spread allocation), OR push ordering into the PostgREST nested select (.order('created_at',{ascending:false}).limit(1) on the embedded recommendations) to also shrink the SSR payload.
4. ErrorBoundary — drop the always-true 'typeof message === "string"' guard (both ternary branches already yield a string); render {message} directly. (Optional: keep if you want defensiveness since error.data is typed any.)

EXPLICITLY NOT IN SCOPE / REJECTED: removing the lazy useState seed + reseed-on-[loaderData] effect. That was flagged by a simplify agent as a 'bandaid' but is a FALSE POSITIVE — useComparisonSubscription mutates comparison/recommendation via setState (realtime image-extraction patches), and the lazy initializer is required for SSR first-render content. Keep as-is.

title: Restore lifestyle fit tags in comparison output
description: 🌶️🌶️🌶️ Highest-priority item from Jasper's 2026-05-25 review (screenshots IMG_2074, IMG_9324). The previous version of the comparison output had lifestyle fit tags that the new version is missing. Scope is narrowed: only the 'lifestyle fit' tags shown in the review screenshot need to be added back — not the broader set of detail fields. Action: (1) identify the exact lifestyle fit tags from the screenshot, (2) update the generation prompt to emit them, (3) update the rendering UI to display them, (4) verify both languages and both viewports.
design:
Final plan (converged after 2 reviewer rounds):

NAME (avoid collision with UserProfile.lifestyle_fit input weights):
  AI output / DB column / TS type all use: lifestyle_fit_comparison

CANONICAL 6 ASPECTS (verbatim from src/lib/comparisonFlow.ts:105-112 + IMG_9324, snake_case English keys; never localized):
  1. proximity_to_cafes
  2. access_to_gym
  3. dog_walking_friendly
  4. quiet_at_night
  5. morning_vs_afternoon_sunlight
  6. laundromat_access

DATA SHAPE (top-level field in AI JSON output):
  lifestyle_fit_comparison: {
    aspect: AspectKey,        // English snake_case, stays English in JA mode
    property_a: string,       // <=80 chars EN / <=50 chars JA, concrete lived-experience
    property_b: string,
    winner?: "A"|"B"|"draw"   // optional
  }[]

JA ASPECT LABELS (UI side, t() lookup):
  proximity_to_cafes -> カフェへの近さ
  access_to_gym -> ジムへのアクセス
  dog_walking_friendly -> 犬の散歩のしやすさ
  quiet_at_night -> 夜間の静かさ
  morning_vs_afternoon_sunlight -> 朝・午後の日当たり
  laundromat_access -> コインランドリーへのアクセス

FILES (ordered):
  1. supabase/migrations/20260526000000_add_recommendations_lifestyle_fit_comparison.sql (NEW)
       ALTER TABLE recommendations ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS lifestyle_fit_comparison jsonb;
  2. supabase/functions/generate-recommendation/index.ts
       - extend AIRecommendation interface with lifestyle_fit_comparison
       - add new prompt section before "## Pros and Cons" instructing the 6 aspects in order
       - extend JSON output example with lifestyle_fit_comparison
       - mirror in JA tail block; instruct "keep aspect keys English snake_case"

  3. src/lib/lifestyleFitAspects.ts (NEW)
       - export ASPECT_ORDER (the 6 keys in order)
       - export AspectKey type
       - export LifestyleFitAspect interface
  4. src/lib/comparisonFlow.ts
       - extend AIRecommendation interface with optional lifestyle_fit_comparison
  5. src/components/compare-result/LifestyleFitGrid.tsx (NEW)
       - props: { aspects, propertyAName, propertyBName }
       - build Map<AspectKey, LifestyleFitAspect> from input, iterate ASPECT_ORDER, render cards
       - grid grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3 matching ScoreCardsGrid
       - mono uppercase label eyebrow, "A · text" / "B · text" lines, optional winner badge reusing ComparisonTable WIN_BADGE/DRAW_BADGE patterns
       - missing aspect -> placeholder card with em-dash; null/empty aspects -> render nothing (parent guards)
       - useTranslation for labels; no emojis
  6. src/components/compare-result/index.ts
       - re-export LifestyleFitGrid
  7. src/pages/ComparisonDetail.tsx
       - extend local AIRecommendation interface with lifestyle_fit_comparison
       - add lifestyle_fit_comparison to the SELECT at line 201
       - in SummaryTab (line 493), import useTranslation, after ComparisonTable render the grid section when non-empty
  8. src/locales/ja/translation.json + src/locales/en/translation.json
       - comparisonDetail.lifestyleFit.title
       - comparisonDetail.lifestyleFit.aspects.{6 keys}
       - comparisonDetail.lifestyleFit.headerA / headerB

TEST STRATEGY (no Vitest/Jest in project):
  - Edge fn smoke: run/extend verify-generate-recommendation.sh for both en and ja; assert lifestyle_fit_comparison present with the 6 known aspect keys; no emoji codepoints in output; JA prose contains JA characters
  - Manual UI QA at localhost:8080: fresh comparison EN -> 6 cards; JA -> JA labels + JA prose; mobile 375px (1 col); desktop 1280px (3 cols); reload persists; pre-existing recommendation (column NULL) renders no section, no crash

OUT OF SCOPE:

title: Individualize comparison page meta (title + description) per property pair
description:
PROBLEM (p0): Every comparison page serves the same <title> and <meta description> regardless of the properties being compared. Current static values:
  Title: 'AiSumai (愛住) - Compare Homes in Japan with AI & Experts'
  Description: 'AiSumai helps renters and home buyers in Japan compare two homes...'
When Google sees many pages with identical content/meta, it indexes only one of them — so building more comparison pages yields almost no incremental search exposure.

FIX: On comparison page load, read both properties' names and prices from the DB and dynamically inject a unique <title> and <meta description>. Example target:
  Title: '世田谷 3LDK vs 目黒 2LDK — Property Comparison | AiSumai'
  Description: 'AI analysis of Setagaya 3LDK (¥68M) vs Meguro 2LDK (¥72M). Compare price, commute, resale outlook. Free on AiSumai.'
This is a React + Vite SPA, so use react-helmet (react-helmet-async) to set per-page meta.

DONE WHEN: Opening two different comparison pages shows different titles in the browser tab.
notes: CORRECTION (2026-06-18): record said this was deferred to nae's RR v7 meta(). In fact the team independently SHIPPED 6za the original react-helmet way on origin/main today (commit 9c254eb): <Helmet> in ComparisonDetail.tsx now sets per-pair <title>/description/og/twitter — exactly this bead's prescribed fix. 6za's 'different tabs show different titles' goal is satisfied CLIENT-SIDE on main. Remains closed. Caveat: client-side helmet meta is not guaranteed server-crawlable; the SSR-crawlable upgrade (Helmet->meta()) is tracked in nae. See also rv5 (crawlability) which this does NOT resolve.

title: analyze-properties catch block references undeclared geminiData, masks real upstream error
description:
The catch block in supabase/functions/analyze-properties/index.ts:982-1000 references a variable 'geminiData' that is never declared in scope. When the try block throws (Gemini parse error, malformed response, etc.), executing the catch raises 'ReferenceError: geminiData is not defined', which gets caught by the outer handler and returned as HTTP 500 with errorType=ReferenceError. The original upstream error is silently lost.

Surfaced 2026-05-27 during browser-driven smoke walkthrough for bead home-duo-insight-2l8. Submitting two real SUUMO URLs via /compare hits analyze-properties and consistently fails with:
  HTTP 500
  {"error":"Internal server error","details":"geminiData is not defined","errorType":"ReferenceError"}

Console: 'Failed to create comparison from landing page: Edge Function returned a non-2xx status code'.

Impact: No new comparison can be created end-to-end through the UI on local Supabase (and presumably also fails on remote when the catch path is hit). Manual smoke walkthroughs and any user trying to compare two properties are blocked.

Likely fix: the in-scope variable inside callGeminiForProperty is 'data' (line 531). The catch block at the call-site doesn't have access to that local. Two options:
  1. Wrap the gemini call so the response object bubbles out: e.g. const geminiData = await callGeminiForProperty(...); then move parse logic into try {} where geminiData is in scope, and the catch can read it.
  2. Remove the geminiData references from the catch block and just log error.message / error.stack.

Acceptance: a fresh /compare submission with two valid SUUMO URLs completes through analyze-properties → generate-recommendation → comparison detail page, with no ReferenceError thrown along the way. When Gemini truly fails (e.g., quota / malformed JSON), the response is a 422 with the actual parse failure detail, not a 500 ReferenceError.

Repro:
  1. Start local stack (npm run supabase:start + functions serve + npm run dev)
  2. Visit http://localhost:8080/compare
  3. Paste any two SUUMO URLs and submit
  4. Observe HTTP 500 in Network panel with details='geminiData is not defined'

Code refs:
  - supabase/functions/analyze-properties/index.ts:986 (geminiDataReceived)
  - supabase/functions/analyze-properties/index.ts:987 (responseText)
  - supabase/functions/analyze-properties/index.ts:993 (geminiResponse)
  - declared as 'data' at supabase/functions/analyze-properties/index.ts:531
design: Replace catch at supabase/functions/analyze-properties/index.ts:982-1000 with: console.error('Error processing extracted property data:', {errorMessage: error.message, errorStack: error.stack}); return 422 with {error, details: error.message} only. Drop geminiDataReceived/responseText from log and geminiResponse field from body. Confirmed no frontend consumer of geminiResponse via grep -r src/. Out of scope: L567 inner gemini catch, L1001 outer catch, 422 status, plumbing gemini state (Option 1).
notes: VERIFIED. Branch: bead-a89-geminidata-refsfix @ b24e8fe (real fix at 8e43e51). Worktree: /Users/neo/Projects/dowell/home-duo-insight/.claude/worktrees/agent-a41707a2385e7f6e7. Build: green (npm run build, vite 6.22s). Lint: 0 errors, 16 pre-existing warnings. ui_surface=false (edge function only). Tests skipped — no unit scaffold for live-Gemini integration. Diff confirmed exactly matches plan: 3 geminiData refs removed from catch L982-1000, log label tightened, geminiResponse field dropped from 422 body (no frontend consumer per grep -r src/). Awaiting human merge to main per project CLAUDE.md rule.

title: Functional toggle chips on /compare form drive which aspects appear in summary_table
description:
On the landing-page /compare form, the existing FILTER_CHIPS strip (price / access / age / layout / school / risk in src/pages/Index.tsx:23-30) is currently visual-only — chips toggle activeChips locally but never feed into generateRecommendation. This bead makes them functional and adds 6 new chips for the lifestyle aspects we just wired into summary_table via bead 2l8.

Goal: each chip on the /compare form toggles whether its aspect appears as a row in the AI-generated summary_table. Chip ON = include row; chip OFF = AI omits the row entirely.

UI scope:
- Add 6 new chips to FILTER_CHIPS for the lifestyle aspects, placed immediately after the existing 6 at the same visual level (small kebab strip, same styling, same toggle behavior):
  - cafe (カフェ) → 'Cafés nearby' / 'カフェへの近さ' row

Plumbing scope:

- Forward enabled_aspects to the edge function payload.

title: ComparisonDetail 404 ErrorBoundary shows English error string regardless of locale
description:
In src/pages/ComparisonDetail.tsx, the SSR loader throws `data("Comparison not found", { status: 404 })` and the ErrorBoundary renders the thrown string directly: `message = isRouteErrorResponse(error) ? error.data : ...` then `{typeof message === "string" ? message : t("comparisonDetail.notFound.description")}`. Because error.data is always the English string, the i18n fallback (comparisonDetail.notFound.description) is dead code, and a JA visitor hitting a dead/stale comparison link sees a localized title but an English body line ('Comparison not found').

Fix: throw a stable machine code (e.g. data("not-found", {status:404})) or ignore error.data in the boundary and always render the localized t('comparisonDetail.notFound.description'). Keep the localized title. Originated from code review of PR #9 (nae).

title: rv5-a: SSR foundation — scaffold React Router v7 framework mode + Vercel preset
description: Foundation step of the Option B SSR migration (see docs/rv5-ssr-design.md). Scaffold React Router v7 framework mode + the @vercel/react-router preset so the app renders server-side on Vercel Functions, WITHOUT changing user-facing behavior. Tasks: install react-router@7 + @react-router/dev + @vercel/react-router; add react-router.config.ts with ssr:true and presets:[vercelPreset()]; set Vite rollupOptions.input for the SSR build/server entry; replace the @vercel/static-build config in vercel.json (or let the preset drive build output); migrate the BrowserRouter route table in src/App.tsx to v7 framework-mode route modules. All 13 existing routes must still work. Riskiest step; blocks all other rv5 children. DONE WHEN: app builds and deploys as SSR on Vercel; every route renders with no regression; view-source of a route shows server-rendered markup.
design: CONVERGED PLAN (rv5-a RR v7 SSR scaffold, plan loop 2 rounds). Approach: RR v7 framework mode (react-router@7.9.4) + @vercel/react-router@1.3.1 vercelPreset(), appDirectory:'src' (keep src/, no app/). NEW: react-router.config.ts {appDirectory:'src',ssr:true,presets:[vercelPreset()]}; src/routes.ts (flat table, 13 routes); src/root.tsx (document shell html/head/body + Meta/Links/Scripts/ScrollRestoration + providers AuthProvider>QueryClientProvider(useState) + conditional Topbar/Footer around Outlet + PageTracker + initGA effect + Toaster); src/entry.client.tsx (hydrateRoot + client-only ./i18n + ./index.css); src/routes/compare-redirect.tsx (client <Navigate to /#compare-widget>). NO entry.server.tsx (Vercel default streaming entry, confirmed). MOD: vite.config.ts (reactRouter() replaces react()/swc, keep @ alias + dev-gated componentTagger + port 8080); package.json scripts dev/build=react-router, start=react-router-serve; DELETE index.html (head→root.tsx incl charset/viewport/lang=en/title/desc/OG/twitter/emoji-favicon/gtag literal script/gptengineer), src/main.tsx, src/App.tsx; REMOVE vercel.json (drops static-build + SPA catch-all). B1 MANDATORY: bump react-router-dom ^6.26.2->^7 (20 import sites resolve to single v7 context, zero edits) — gate: npm ls shows single deduped v7, no v6. B2: rm bun.lockb, npm install regen package-lock.json (npm-only). i18n: server lng:'en' no detector / client detector upgrades (mismatch-free; KNOWN regression: JA EN-flash + crawl-lang, accepted for scaffold, fast-follow via root loader). SSR-safe verified: AuthProvider(effect-scoped), use-mobile(useState undefined init), RecommendationFeedback/ComparisonDetail(effect-scoped). OUT: loaders, per-page meta, sitemap, image-opt, OG cards, prod deploy. Full plan in dev brief.
notes: Vercel project created + deployed (koromikos-projects/home-duo-insight, prod alias https://home-duo-insight-six.vercel.app). Root cause: react-helmet-async + lucide-react ship ESM entry as ambiguous .js (import condition) with no type:module / no nested dist/esm package.json; Vercel's serverless loader treats it as CJS and can't see named exports (works locally via Node ESM syntax-detection). title: Ensure Google can crawl/index comparison page content (SSR/prerender)
description:
PROBLEM: Comparison page content is rendered entirely client-side via JavaScript. Google must do a second rendering pass to see it, which sometimes fails and often slows indexing — limiting search visibility.

HOW TO VERIFY THE PROBLEM: Google Search Console → URL Inspection → paste any comparison page URL → click 'Test Live URL' → check whether the rendered screenshot shows the full comparison content or a blank page.

FIX (this is a React + Vite SPA, NOT Next.js):
  - Evaluate adding an SSR/prerender layer for comparison routes — e.g. prerender.io as an SSR proxy, or a build-time/edge prerender so the served HTML already contains the property data.
  - Alternatively, scope and propose a migration to an SSR-capable framework (e.g. Next.js, where comparison pages would use server-side rendering) as a larger follow-up.
  (For reference: on Next.js the fix would be getServerSideProps so the HTML includes property data server-side.)

DONE WHEN: GSC Live URL test for a comparison page shows full comparison content in the rendered HTML/screenshot, not a blank page.
design: See docs/rv5-ssr-design.md for full analysis. RECOMMENDATION: React Router v7 framework mode + @vercel/react-router preset (ssr:true) — only option that solves rv5 (crawlable SSR HTML) AND 6za (per-route meta() = per-pair title/desc) in one mechanism, keeps react-router, real SSR (no cloaking/3rd-party renderer). Effort: L. Stopgap alt: prerender.io via Vercel Edge Middleware (S-M) but leaves 6za unsolved + needs window.prerenderReady signal. Avoid Next.js (XL) — its headline ISR barely matters since comparison data is write-once; only Next gaps that bite RR v7 are image optimization + OG images, both with Vercel workarounds. Comparison data confirmed anon-readable (no auth plumbing). OPEN DECISIONS: (1) RR v7 now vs prerender.io stopgap; (2) GSC property owner; (3) SSR comparison-only vs all public routes. rv5 should not ship independently of 6za.
notes: Decision 2026-06-18: pursuing Option B (React Router v7 framework mode) per docs/rv5-ssr-design.md. Split into children: dn4 (foundation, P0) -> nae (comparison SSR + meta, P0, absorbs 6za) + xeq (other public routes). rv5 closes when dn4+nae+xeq done. SEO enhancements rqm (sitemap), cyv (image opt), mug (OG cards) tracked under epic 1rk. 6za closed/absorbed into nae.

design: Add nullable columns on properties. Consider grouping rarely-set fields in an amenities jsonb blob to keep the migration narrow. Each field nullable so existing rows are valid. Update supabase/migrations + regenerate types (see types regeneration bead).
acceptance_criteria: Migration committed. New columns queryable. src/integrations/supabase/types.ts updated. DetailsTab schema can reference them.

title: Edge fn analyze-properties: extract extended property fields
description: Update supabase/functions/analyze-properties to pull the fields added in D1 (building structure, total units, management company, parking, concierge, pet policy, seismic standard, etc.) from SUUMO pages via Gemini.
design: Expand Gemini schema. Keep tolerant to missing fields (nulls OK). Document field sources per SUUMO section.
acceptance_criteria: Re-running analyze-properties on test URLs (provided in session memory) populates ≥60% of new fields. Rows with missing fields remain valid.

title: DetailsTab: render full 14-row mockup table
description: Replace the current 8-row DetailsTab in src/pages/ComparisonDetail.tsx with the mockup's canonical 14 rows. Rows with NULL data render '—'. Use ComparisonRow winner+badge where sensible (e.g. 管理費安, ペット可).
design: Row list should mirror refactor-plan/aisumai-compare-result.html lines 690-704. Group highlight/non-highlight alternation already handled by ComparisonTable? If not, add per-row hint.
acceptance_criteria: Details tab shows all 14 rows in the mockup order. Empty fields show '—'. Visual matches mockup.

title: Expert section: unclaimed state parity with mockup
description: Mockup unclaimed state (refactor-plan/aisumai-compare-result.html lines 606-626): dashed-border box, eu-icon, eu-label '不動産専門家の方へ', eu-title, eu-sub, 'このレポートを認領する →' CTA, footer row with view/save stats + area. Verify src/components/compare-result/ExpertSectionPanel.tsx and underlying ExpertSection render this layout; close gaps.
acceptance_criteria: Logged-out expert view visually matches mockup unclaimed block. Accessibility checked.

title: Expert section: claimed state parity with mockup
description: Mockup claimed state (lines 629-680): expert profile header (ec-avatar initial, name, role, badges incl. 認証済み/エリア専門/外国人対応, deals count), verdict block (ec-verdict 19px serif), paragraphs, ec-points (rec-b, caution), contact CTA with 'ログインして相談する →' gated by login.
acceptance_criteria: Displayed expert block includes avatar, role, verified+specialty+language badges, deals count, structured comment points, and login-gated contact CTA. Matches mockup.

title: SimilarProperties: 4-card section below tabs
description: Mockup similar-section (lines 825-837) renders 4 cards of 'similar condition' properties. Not present today. Create src/components/compare-result/SimilarProperties.tsx and query supabase for properties matching prefecture/price band; mount between CompareTabs and ExpertSectionPanel.
design: Query: same prefecture as property_a or property_b, price within ±20%, limit 4, exclude the two compared. If fewer than 4 results, hide section. Grid: 4 cols desktop, 2 cols mobile, using design tokens.
acceptance_criteria: Section appears when ≥4 matches exist. Links to /comparisons or /feed as appropriate. Matches mockup visual.

title: PDF export: 'PDF保存' button for comparison report
description: Mockup topbar has a 'PDF保存' button next to Share. Current implementation has only share. Add PDF export that renders the comparison detail (hero + tabs + AI analysis) to a downloadable PDF.
design: Option 1: client-side html2pdf/print CSS. Option 2: server-side via puppeteer edge fn. Recommend print-CSS first for simplicity; progressive enhancement later.
acceptance_criteria: Clicking 'PDF保存' downloads a PDF with property names, scores, summary table, and AI analysis. Page layout legible in A4 portrait.

title: Sticky bar: translucent paper style + verdict line
description: Current sticky bar (ComparisonDetail.tsx:335-355) is ink-black with paper buttons. Mockup (lines 308-313, 843-857) uses translucent paper (rgba(249,248,245,0.95)+blur), ghost + solid buttons, plus a 'sticky-props' line showing 'A name vs B name — AI推奨: 物件X'.
design: Swap classes to bg-paper/95 backdrop-blur + border-t border-rule. Left: property names + verdict (use recommendation winner when available). Right: '← 新しく比較', 'シェア', '専門家に相談' buttons using .btn-ghost/.btn-solid equivalents.
acceptance_criteria: Sticky bar matches mockup color treatment and contents. Verdict line omitted gracefully when no recommendation.

title: Breadcrumb: align Japanese copy with mockup
description: Current breadcrumb renders English: 'Feed / Compare Report' (ComparisonDetail.tsx:242-246). Mockup uses 'Home / 比較 / <A> vs <B>' (refactor-plan lines 350-357). Update to Japanese labels and include property names in the final segment.
acceptance_criteria: Breadcrumb reads 'Home / 比較 / ${A} vs ${B}' in all supported locales, falling back to English keys if translations missing.

title: Edge fn generate-recommendation: emit AI axis scores
description: Update supabase/functions/generate-recommendation to produce total + per-axis (価格/立地/建物) scores for both properties. Also set the winner (A/B/draw) based on total so the detail page stops using the price heuristic.
design: Gemini prompt update + response parsing. Scores 0-100 integers. Persist into the new columns from the schema bead.
acceptance_criteria: New comparisons get populated scores. Re-running the function on an existing comparison upserts scores. Unit script verifies shape.

title: Decide: keep or remove ProsConsGrid from SummaryTab
description: ProsConsGrid (src/components/compare-result/ProsConsGrid.tsx) renders per-property pros/cons. Mockup has no counterpart — it relies on AI points (Cluster C) + expert points (Cluster E) for that information, which makes ProsConsGrid redundant after those land. Decide: (a) keep pros/cons as extra value beyond mockup, or (b) drop them once C ships. If (b), delete component + export from src/components/compare-result/index.ts and remove from SummaryTab.
acceptance_criteria: Decision recorded in bead notes. If (b), PR removing ProsConsGrid is merged; if (a), brief rationale documented.
notes: Decision: REMOVE ProsConsGrid after tv7.10 lands. Why: once structured AI points (tv7.8/tv7.9) + expert points render in SummaryTab, ProsConsGrid is redundant. How to apply: delete src/components/compare-result/ProsConsGrid.tsx + index export + SummaryTab mount in the PR that wires tv7.10.

title: Topbar: verify parity with mockup (logo, nav, share/PDF)
description: Mockup has a fixed topbar (lines 333-345) with 愛住 AiSumai logo + Beta pill, nav (比較/物件一覧/エリア/About), and action buttons (シェア + PDF保存). Current app shell is not shown in ComparisonDetail.tsx — verify the shared topbar matches these requirements (possibly in src/components layout). File gaps as sub-tasks.
acceptance_criteria: Topbar nav/logo/actions match mockup, or follow-up beads are filed for specific gaps.
notes: Verified src/components/Header.tsx. Matches mockup: 愛住 AiSumai logo + Beta pill (OK), nav 4 items: /compare(比較) /feed(物件一覧) /experts /about(About). Gaps: (a) mockup has エリア instead of /experts — minor, decide in follow-up; (b) mockup has シェア + PDF保存 action buttons which are page-specific (covered by tv7.17 PDF export and tv7.18 sticky bar). No new sub-beads filed; existing beads cover the gaps.

title: Regenerate Supabase types after A1/B2/C1/D1 migrations
description: After each schema-changing bead lands (A1 scores, B2 summary_table shape, C1 ai_points, D1 property fields), run npx supabase gen types typescript to refresh src/integrations/supabase/types.ts. Can be run once at the end or after each merge.
acceptance_criteria: types.ts reflects the new columns; TypeScript build passes; frontend consumers can read the new shapes without 'any'.

title: WinnerBanner: sub-meta, total score, axis bars, reason
description: Current WinnerBanner (src/components/compare-result/WinnerBanner.tsx) shows only label/name/price. Mockup (score-duel) adds: sub-meta line (area · floor · age), large 64px total score, 3 axis bars with numeric labels, winner-side reason paragraph + 'AI 推奨' crown.
design: Props: add scoreTotal?, axisScores?: {price, location, building}, reason?, subMeta?. Reuse .score-duel / .duel-side CSS. Animate bars via existing bar-fill keyframe (tailwind.config.ts). Keep fallback rendering when scores missing.
acceptance_criteria: When scores are present: renders total, 3 animated bars with numeric labels, reason on winner. When absent: current minimal layout. Snapshot matches score-duel block in refactor-plan/aisumai-compare-result.html lines 367-420.

title: ScoreCardsGrid: 3-card A-vs-B overview component
description: Mockup Overview tab has a 3-column score-cards block (価格・コスト / 立地・交通 / 建物・設備), each with 2 paired A/B bars + legend. Not implemented today. Create new src/components/compare-result/ScoreCardsGrid.tsx and mount at the top of SummaryTab.
design: Props: cards: [{ label, metrics: [{ name, aLabel, bLabel, aPct, bPct }] }]. Use existing tokens (text-label-sm, rule, ink/paper). Legend row at bottom of each card. Animate bars via bar-fill keyframe.
acceptance_criteria: Overview tab shows the 3 cards above the comparison table when scoring data is available. Mirrors layout in refactor-plan/aisumai-compare-result.html lines 434-493.

title: ComparisonRow: add winner + badge fields and render
description: Mockup comparison table shows per-row win-badge (安/広/近/多/新/割安/日当り/可/高…) and a 'draw-badge' variant. Current ComparisonTable (src/components/compare-result/ComparisonTable.tsx) has no winner concept. Extend ComparisonRow type with { winner?: 'A'|'B'|'draw', badgeLabel?: string } and render the pill.
design: Add win-badge (ink bg, paper fg, font-mono 8px) on the winning cell per mockup lines 123-125. Draw variant uses paper-dark. Pure FE work; no data dependency on its own.
acceptance_criteria: ComparisonTable renders inline pill on the winner cell when props supplied. Accessible label (sr-only 'winner' text). No regression when fields absent.

title: Edge fn generate-recommendation: emit per-row winner + badge label
description: Extend recommendations.summary_table schema so each row carries winner ('A'|'B'|'draw') and a short badge label (e.g. '安','広','近','多','新'). Today rows are { field, property_a, property_b }.
design: Row shape: { field, property_a, property_b, winner?, badge? }. Prompt the model to choose a 1-3 char badge matching the winner. Keep migration back-compatible — consumer treats fields as optional.
acceptance_criteria: New recommendations return rows with winner/badge. Frontend ComparisonRow consumes them.

title: SummaryTab: wire winner/badge from recommendation.summary_table into ComparisonTable
description: In src/pages/ComparisonDetail.tsx SummaryTab, map recommendation.summary_table[i].winner/badge into ComparisonRow props once B1+B2 land.
acceptance_criteria: When recommendation data includes winner+badge, summary table cells render the win-badge. Verified against a regenerated comparison.

title: Edge fn generate-recommendation: emit structured AI points
description: Mockup AI analysis block has 3 colored points (pro-a / pro-b / warn) with icons (◇ ◆ ⚠). Current recommendations have only final_recommendation (prose) and pros/cons arrays. Emit a typed points[] so AIAnalysisBlock can render them (AIPoint type already defined in src/components/compare-result).

title: AIAnalysisBlock: add model badge + disclaimer + structured points rendering
description: Mockup ai-analysis block (refactor-plan/aisumai-compare-result.html lines 564-592) has: dark header with 'AI Analysis' eyebrow + title + model badge ('Claude Sonnet 4'), markdown body, 3 colored points, footer disclaimer. Today AIAnalysisBlock renders only body + optional points. Add headerTitle, headerEyebrow, modelBadge, disclaimer props and ensure .ai-point pro-a/pro-b/warn styling matches.
design: Preserve existing ink-inverted dark header. Points list: icon + bold prefix + body. Disclaimer uses label-mono 9px, ink-30. All new props optional; preserve current call sites.
acceptance_criteria: Block visually matches mockup when given all props. Backward compatible with current usage in SummaryTab.

title: analyze-properties: drop unused getSession() call

Wire area from properties.address first 3 chars or a prefecture column if/when added.

title: rv5-c: SSR remaining public routes (home, feed, experts, expert profile, about)
description: Extend SSR to the remaining indexable public routes: /, /feed, /experts, /experts/:expertId, /about. Each becomes a v7 route module with loader() (server Supabase fetch where needed) + meta(). Leave /auth, /dashboard, /admin/* client-only (non-indexable). DONE WHEN: view-source of each route shows server-rendered content + route-appropriate meta.
design: SSR-convert 5 public routes in worktree off nae branch. Pattern = nae ComparisonDetail (react-router imports; anon supabase in loader; LoaderFunctionArgs/MetaArgs/HeadersArgs; throw data(..,{status:404,Cache-Control:no-store}); headers()->public,s-maxage=3600,swr=86400; ErrorBoundary via useRouteError/isRouteErrorResponse; reseed useState(()=>loaderData.x)+useEffect([loaderData])). Meta strings STATIC ENGLISH (i18n pins lng=en on SSR). Use SITE_URL/OG_IMAGE_URL from @/lib/site. ROUTES: (1)About.tsx meta-only, swap rr-dom->rr. (2)Index.tsx meta-only (useEffect@108 is hash-scroll, content static JSX), canonical=/. (3)Experts.tsx META-ONLY (NO public list; page SSRs the AUTH-GATE sign-in card for anon, hero/stats are auth-gated client-only) -> meta copy describes the 'become a verified expert/apply' FUNNEL not a directory; do NOT refactor auth-gating (follow-up bead). (4)Feed.tsx loader()+meta()+headers(): move fetchComparisons body server-side incl votes/experts Promise.all enrichment, return {comparisons}; seed useState from loaderData + reseed effect; isLoading init false; CRITICAL guard existing fetch effect with 'if(refreshTrigger===0)return;' so SSR data isn't overwritten on mount (retry button bumps trigger>0 -> still works; t-dep relang skipped); filters/pagination stay client; no ErrorBoundary. (5)ExpertProfilePage.tsx loader()+meta()+headers()+ErrorBoundary(): fetch expert_profiles.eq(id).single()+votes count, throw 404 if missing, return {expertProfile,activity,seo}; dynamic title=expert NAME; meta 404-guard returns single title; remove isLoading skeleton + in-component 404 block (loader throws); KEEP fetchExpertProfile client refetch + isOwnProfile from useAuth (NOT loader). Add Feed dateAgo clock-skew comment. routes.ts/root.tsx/site.ts/vite.config UNCHANGED. OUT OF SCOPE: image-opt(cyv),OG(mug),helmet removal(p96),Feed N+1. Verify: npm run build green (deploy gate) + npm run start view-source per route shows content+exactly-one correct title; garbage expert id->404+no-store; no hydration warnings; npm run lint.
notes: PR OPENED: https://github.com/pptang/home-duo-insight/pull/10 (base main, head feat/home-duo-insight-xeq-ssr-public-routes @ 775387b). Rebased onto main AFTER nae #9 merged (4c7df0b) — clean, no conflicts, integrated build/tsc/lint all green (re-verified on rebased state). 5 public routes SSR'd (About/Index/Experts meta-only; Feed + ExpertProfilePage loader+meta+headers; ExpertProfilePage adds ErrorBoundary 404). Shared buildMeta() in src/lib/seo.ts; truncate in src/lib/format.ts. QA all PASS (view-source content + per-route single titles; /feed 20 real rows; Paipo!=Mashimashi dynamic titles; garbage id->404; 0 hydration warnings). Escalations: none. AWAITING #10 MERGE — bd close after merge to main.
close_reason: rv5-c SSR public routes (home/feed/experts/expert-profile/about) merged to main via PR #10
